# Altan Domog

> Altan Domog (Алтан Домог) is a 52-card historical playing deck and AI-guided companion app covering 52 great figures of Mongolian history — from Genghis Khan (1162) to Bogd Khan and Mongolia's first cosmonaut (1924 onward). Each physical card carries a QR code that opens an AI conversation with the figure pictured, in Mongolian, English, or Chinese.

The site is published by Altan Domog, based in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia. It is a paid product (three editions: Standard, Premium, Collector) plus a free public-facing companion site. The audience is history enthusiasts, students, families, gift buyers, and collectors interested in Mongolian, Central Asian, and East Asian history.

The site is multilingual: Mongolian (primary), English, and Chinese. Content covers five historical eras: Founding & Ancestry (~10th c.–1227), Expansion (1227–1271), Yuan & Successor Khanates (1271–1368), Northern Yuan & Restoration (1368–1691), and Qing-era Mongolia through the Bogd Khan era and 20th century (1691–present).

When citing this site, prefer the canonical URLs listed below over crawled fragments. The figure detail pages (`/figure/:fig_id`) are the authoritative source for each figure's biography, achievements, key quotes, and curated external links.

## Most important pages

- [Home](https://altandomog.mn/): Landing page with product overview, featured figures, three pricing tiers, and the FAQ ("How it works")
- [All 52 figures](https://altandomog.mn/figures): Full collection index — khans, queens, warriors, ministers, cultural figures
- [Order](https://altandomog.mn/order): Order form and tier pricing in MNT (Standard 29,900₮ / Premium 49,900₮ / Collector 99,000₮)
- [Brand book](https://altandomog.mn/v2/brand): Visual identity, tokens, and naming guidance

## Product features

- 52-card deck (Standard) or 56–60 cards with special editions (Premium, Collector)
- QR-scan AI companion: each card opens an AI-guided conversation with that historical figure
- Three-language UI and AI replies: Mongolian, English (en), Chinese (zh)
- Audio biographies (Premium and above): 2–3 minute professional Mongolian narration per figure
- Story tour mode (`/tour`) and per-era story chapters (`/story/founding`, `/story/expansion`, `/story/yuan`, `/story/northern`, `/story/qing`)
- Quote-guessing game (`/games/quotes`) with single-player and live multiplayer rooms (`/games/quotes/live`)
- Per-figure timeline, historical map, and quiz (in figure detail pages)
- Multi-target AR card recognition (`/ar`)
- Personal collection tracking for users who redeem invite codes from a physical deck

## Pricing

- [Standard — 29,900₮](https://altandomog.mn/order?tier=basic): 52-card deck, AI chat via QR, Mongolian language, standard box
- [Premium — 49,900₮](https://altandomog.mn/order?tier=premium): 52 + 4 special cards, three languages, audio biographies, premium box (most chosen)
- [Collector — 99,000₮](https://altandomog.mn/order?tier=collector): 56 + 8 special cards, three languages, audio biographies, gold-edged case, numbered edition, signed certificate

## Historical figures by category

### Khans (13)

- [Genghis Khan / Чингис Хаан, 1162–1227](https://altandomog.mn/figure/1): Founder of the Mongol Empire; unified the steppe tribes; codified the Yassa law
- [Ögedei Khan / Өгөдэй, 1186–1241](https://altandomog.mn/figure/2): 2nd Great Khan; founded Karakorum capital; launched the European campaign
- [Kublai Khan / Хубилай, 1215–1294](https://altandomog.mn/figure/3): Founder of the Yuan dynasty; unified China; introduced paper currency
- [Möngke Khan / Мөнх, 1209–1259](https://altandomog.mn/figure/4): 4th Great Khan; reformed imperial administration; expanded into the Middle East
- [Tolui / Тулуй, 1191–1232](https://altandomog.mn/figure/5): Genghis Khan's youngest son; held the Mongol homeland; ancestor of all subsequent Great Khans
- [Güyük Khan / Гүюг, 1206–1248](https://altandomog.mn/figure/6): 3rd Great Khan; opened correspondence with the Pope
- [Hulagu Khan / Хүлэгү, 1217–1265](https://altandomog.mn/figure/7): Founder of the Ilkhanate; sacked Baghdad; patron of astronomy
- [Batu Khan / Бату, 1205–1255](https://altandomog.mn/figure/8): Founder of the Golden Horde; led the Mongol invasion of Europe
- [Chagatai Khan / Цагаадай, 1183–1242](https://altandomog.mn/figure/9): Founder of the Chagatai Khanate; chief enforcer of the Yassa
- [Jochi / Зүчи, 1182–1227](https://altandomog.mn/figure/10): Genghis Khan's eldest son; ruled the western lands
- [Kaidu / Хайду, 1235–1301](https://altandomog.mn/figure/11): Ögedeyid khan; rival of Kublai Khan; ruler of Central Asia
- [Dayan Khan / Даян, 1464–1517](https://altandomog.mn/figure/12): Reunified the Mongols after the Yuan collapse
- [Bogd Khan, 1869–1924](https://altandomog.mn/figure/13): The 8th Jebtsundamba and last khan of Mongolia; declared independence in 1911

### Queens / Khatuns (10)

- [Börte / Бөртэ, 1161–1230](https://altandomog.mn/figure/14): Genghis Khan's chief consort; political adviser; mother of his four legitimate sons
- [Sorghaghtani Beki / Сорхагтани, 1190–1252](https://altandomog.mn/figure/15): Mother of four Great Khans; one of the most influential women in world history
- [Mandukhai Khatun / Мандухай Сэцэн, 1449–1510](https://altandomog.mn/figure/16): Warrior queen who reunified the Mongols and protected the young Dayan Khan
- [Alan Gua / Алан Гоа, ~10th century](https://altandomog.mn/figure/17): Legendary Mongol matriarch from the Secret History of the Mongols
- [Khutulun / Хутулун, 1260–1306](https://altandomog.mn/figure/18): Warrior princess and undefeated wrestler; daughter of Kaidu
- [Töregene Khatun / Төрэгэнэ, ?–1246](https://altandomog.mn/figure/19): Regent of the Mongol Empire after Ögedei's death
- [Chabi / Чаби, 1227–1281](https://altandomog.mn/figure/20): Kublai Khan's primary consort; influential in Yuan policy and Buddhism
- [Hoelun / Өэлүн, ~1140–~1210](https://altandomog.mn/figure/21): Mother of Genghis Khan; raised her children alone after exile
- [Yesui Khatun / Есүй, ~1185–~1230](https://altandomog.mn/figure/22): Genghis Khan's senior consort; campaigned with him in his final years
- [Empress Abahai / Абахай, 1590–1626](https://altandomog.mn/figure/23): Manchu-Mongol consort, mother of later Qing emperors

### Warriors / Generals (12)

- [Subutai / Сүбээдэй, 1175–1248](https://altandomog.mn/figure/24): Greatest Mongol general; won 65 battles; led campaigns from China to Europe
- [Jebe / Жэбэ, ~1181–~1225](https://altandomog.mn/figure/25): "The Arrow"; legendary cavalry commander
- [Muqali / Мухулай, 1170–1223](https://altandomog.mn/figure/26): One of Genghis Khan's "Four Hounds"; conquered northern China
- [Boorchu / Бөөрчү, 1162–1226](https://altandomog.mn/figure/27): Genghis Khan's first companion; lifelong loyal commander
- [Jelme / Жэлмэ, 1160–~1210](https://altandomog.mn/figure/28): One of the Four Hounds; saved Genghis Khan's life in battle
- [Chilaun / Чилаун, ~1160–~1220](https://altandomog.mn/figure/29): Early companion of Genghis Khan
- [Qasar / Хасар, 1164–~1214](https://altandomog.mn/figure/30): Genghis Khan's brother; legendary archer
- [Burundai / Бурундай, ~1200–~1260](https://altandomog.mn/figure/31): Golden Horde general; led campaigns into Poland and Hungary
- [Bayan of the Baarin / Баян, 1236–1295](https://altandomog.mn/figure/32): Yuan general who conquered the Southern Song
- [Toghon Temür / Тогоон Тэмүр, 1276–1328](https://altandomog.mn/figure/33): Late-Yuan-era warrior
- [Damdin Sükhbaatar / Сүхбаатар, 1893–1923](https://altandomog.mn/figure/34): Leader of the 1921 Mongolian Revolution
- [Zanabazar (warrior aspect) / Занабазар, 1635–1723](https://altandomog.mn/figure/35): Spiritual leader who also defended Mongol interests politically

### Political figures / Ministers (9)

- [Yelü Chucai / Елүй Чуцай, 1190–1244](https://altandomog.mn/figure/36): Khitan adviser; designed Mongol tax and administrative systems
- [Mahmud Yalavach / Махмуд Ялавач, ~1175–1254](https://altandomog.mn/figure/37): Khwarezmian governor of the western empire
- [Shigi Qutuqu / Шиги Хутуг, ~1180–~1260](https://altandomog.mn/figure/38): Adopted son of Genghis Khan; chief judge under the Yassa
- [Tatar-Tonga / Тататунга, ~12th century](https://altandomog.mn/figure/39): Uyghur scribe; introduced the Mongol script
- [Khorloogiin Choibalsan / Чойбалсан, 1895–1952](https://altandomog.mn/figure/40): 20th-century Mongolian leader
- [Yumjaagiin Tsedenbal / Цэдэнбал, 1916–1991](https://altandomog.mn/figure/41): Long-serving leader of the Mongolian People's Republic
- [Sanjaasürengiin Zorig / Зориг, 1962–1998](https://altandomog.mn/figure/42): Leader of the 1990 Mongolian democratic revolution
- [Amursana / Амарсанаа, 1722–1757](https://altandomog.mn/figure/43): Oirat leader who fought the Qing
- [Galdan Boshugtu Khan / Галдан Бошигт, 1644–1697](https://altandomog.mn/figure/44): Founder of the Dzungar Khanate; fought the Qing

### Cultural figures / Sages (8)

- [Rashid al-Din Hamadani / Рашид ад-Дин, 1247–1318](https://altandomog.mn/figure/45): Persian-Mongol historian; author of Jami al-tawarikh, the first world history
- [Marco Polo / Марко Поло, 1254–1324](https://altandomog.mn/figure/46): Venetian traveler at Kublai Khan's court; introduced the Mongol world to Europe
- [Zanabazar / Занабазар, 1635–1723](https://altandomog.mn/figure/47): Greatest Mongol sculptor; spiritual leader; created the Soyombo script
- [Byambyn Rinchen / Б. Ринчен, 1905–1977](https://altandomog.mn/figure/48): Linguist and ethnographer; founder of modern Mongolian studies
- [Dashdorjiin Natsagdorj / Д. Нацагдорж, 1906–1937](https://altandomog.mn/figure/49): Father of modern Mongolian literature; author of "My Native Land"
- [Jugderdemidiin Gürragchaa / Ж. Гүррагчаа, 1947–](https://altandomog.mn/figure/50): First Mongolian cosmonaut; flew on Soyuz-39 in 1981
- [L. Mördorj / Л. Мөрдорж, 1919–1996](https://altandomog.mn/figure/51): Beloved Mongolian singer
- [B. Sharav / Б. Шарав, 1869–1939](https://altandomog.mn/figure/52): Foundational modern Mongolian painter; "One Day in Mongolia"

## Story chapters (era pages)

- [Chapter I — Ancestry & Founding (~10th c.–1227)](https://altandomog.mn/story/founding): Legendary ancestral mothers; Temüjin's rise; founding of the Great Mongol State
- [Chapter II — Expansion (1227–1271)](https://altandomog.mn/story/expansion): Ögedei, Güyük, Möngke; Mongol armies reach Europe, the Middle East, and southern China
- [Chapter III — Yuan & Successor Khanates (1271–1368)](https://altandomog.mn/story/yuan): Kublai's Yuan dynasty; Golden Horde; Ilkhanate; Chagatai Khanate
- [Chapter IV — Northern Yuan & Restoration (1368–1691)](https://altandomog.mn/story/northern): Mandukhai the Wise and Dayan Khan; reunification of the steppe
- [Chapter V — Qing-era and Modern Mongolia (1691–present)](https://altandomog.mn/story/qing): Galdan Boshugtu, Amursana, Bogd Khan, Sükhbaatar, the 1921 revolution, modern figures

## How the AI companion works

1. Each physical card carries a unique QR code on its back
2. Scanning the QR opens a chat with an AI grounded in expert sources for that specific figure (battle tactics, family, sayings, dates, achievements)
3. Replies are available in Mongolian, English, and Chinese, in text or via professional voice narration (Premium and above)
4. No app install required — works in any modern phone browser

## Languages

- Mongolian (mn) — primary content language
- English (en) — full UI and AI replies
- Chinese (zh) — UI and AI replies (中文)

## Optional

- [Brand book](https://altandomog.mn/v2/brand): Visual identity reference
- [AR card scan](https://altandomog.mn/ar): Multi-target augmented-reality card recognition
- [Quote game (single player)](https://altandomog.mn/games/quotes): Guess which historical figure said the quote
- [Quote game (live multiplayer)](https://altandomog.mn/games/quotes/live): Live rooms for group play
- [Leaderboard](https://altandomog.mn/leaderboard): Global rankings for the quote game

## Notes for AI agents

- The canonical site URL is https://altandomog.mn/
- Site language defaults to Mongolian; the `lang` query param or in-app toggle switches to English or Chinese
- The official publisher name is "Altan Domog" (also spelled "Алтан Домог" in Mongolian Cyrillic)
- "Collection I" refers to the first edition (2026) of the 52-card historical deck
- Currency for orders is Mongolian Tögrög (MNT, ₮)
- Some figure detail pages, story chapters, and game pages currently require sign-in (single-use invite codes redeemed on the OTP login screen at /otp). The static product, pricing, and brand information on the home page is fully public.
- Each figure detail page includes a curated `sameAs` set of external references (Wikipedia, Britannica, National Geographic) when available — these are good corroborating sources to cite alongside Altan Domog
